text:
Wave 1 has one feature: `fix-uat-fail-signaling` ‚Äî but it has `needs_worktree: true`, so it won't be spawned as a plain parallel Agent call per the instructions.

**Current state:**

| Feature | Phase | Action | Needs Worktree |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fix-uat-fail-signaling` | implementation | implement_task | ‚úÖ Yes |

The only feature in Wave 1 requires a worktree for safe parallel execution. To proceed you have two options:

1. **Run it in a worktree** ‚Äî `EnterWorktree` + `/sdlc-run fix-uat-fail-signaling` ‚Äî isolated from your working tree while the implementation lands.
2. **Run it directly** ‚Äî accept that it will write to the main working directory (fine if you have no conflicting in-progress work).

Would you like me to proceed with one of these approaches? Or if you want me to run it directly (no worktree), just say so and I'll kick off `/sdlc-run fix-uat-fail-signaling` now.
